product: 115XT (legacy L-Acoustics XT LINE coaxial loudspeaker; discontinued. Distinct from 115XT HiQ. Shares its Operator Manual with the 112XT and 115XT HiQ.)

electroacoustic.max_spl_note: Peak SPL @ 1 m, single enclosure, 3W preset. FRONT preset: 122 dB cont / 128 dB peak; FILL preset: 124 dB cont / 130 dB peak; 3W preset: 126 dB cont / 132 dB peak.
electroacoustic.directivity: Axi-symmetrical 80° conical (±20°), identical horizontal and vertical coverage; crossover 1 kHz, 24 dB/oct Linkwitz-Riley
electroacoustic.components: 1x 15" weather-resistant LF cone (3" voice coil) coaxially loading 1x 1.4" exit neodymium HF compression driver (titanium diaphragm, 3" voice coil); bass-reflex loaded. LF section: 250 Wrms / 1000 Wpeak (8 ohm). HF section: 85 Wrms / 350 Wpeak (8 ohm).

operating_modes: Active 2-way (LF + HF, one amp channel each)
Active 3-way with companion subwoofer (3W closely-coupled or 3WX flown-top/ground-sub)
amplification.preset_note: Legacy presets per the XT Operator Manual were authored for outboard processors (BSS Minidrive, XTA, Lake/Dolby). In the current L-Acoustics LA-network ecosystem the 115XT is driven by an LA4/LA4X amplified controller; one LA4 powers up to 4 enclosures.
amplification.recommended_amplifiers_note: Modern L-Acoustics mapping uses the LA4/LA4X amplified controller. The original-era manual cited LA15a/LA17a/LA24a/LA48a analog amplifiers, all long discontinued.

connection.input_connector: 2x 4-point speakON (NL4), internally wired in parallel for loop-through / parallel operation. Pinout: 1+/1- = LF, 2+/2- = HF.
connection.cabling: Supplied/recommended with 4-conductor SP-series speakON cables (4 mm² / 11 AWG): SP.7 link (0.7 m), SP7 (7 m), SP25 (25 m); CC4FP female/female barrel adapter.

physical.dimensions_mm: 580 H x 446 W (front) / 169 W (rear) x 478 D
physical.cabinet: 18 mm and 30 mm Baltic birch plywood with internal steel bracing; sealed, screwed and rabbeted joints
physical.grille: Black powder-coated 1.5 mm perforated steel with acoustically-transparent grille cloth
physical.shape: Truncated wedge with curved front profile; 41° baffle angle when laid on rear side for floor-monitor use

modes: Pole-mount on subwoofer or stand (36 mm / 1.42" sockets, top and bottom)
Flown via ETR115XT adjustable U-bracket (horizontal or vertical, 10° resolution)
Single-point flown via XTLIFTBAR (5 pick points for tilt)
deployment.max_per_flown_array: Up to 2 enclosures flown vertically (horizontal orientation) from one rigging point using ETR115XT + CPL115XT
rigging_accessories: CPL115XT (coupling adapter bars, 2 pcs, to array two 115XT)
PION2 (double-stud Aeroquip fitting for side flytrack, secondary safety only)
deployment.safety_factor: Ultimate safety factor >12 for the 115XT and all listed rigging accessories

usage: Active two-way 15"/1.4" coaxial point source for distributed sound reinforcement: delay rings, surround/effects channels, fill, and speech delays in convention and sports venues.
Each cabinet needs 2 amp channels (LF + HF); a single LA4/LA4X drives up to 4 enclosures, so a 4-box system fits one amplified controller.
Wedge format with a 41° baffle plus the MONITOR preset makes it a high-output stage floor monitor; the coaxial design gives even, lobe-free coverage and good feedback rejection.
For full-range music use the FRONT preset (shelved contours); use the FILL preset for flat response in speech and close-proximity fill.

Pair with an SB115, SB118, SB218 or dV-SUB sub for 3-way operation: pick 3W presets (100 Hz crossover) for closely-coupled stacks or 3WX presets (80 Hz LP on sub, 115XT extended to 50 Hz) when the top is flown over ground subs.
Up to two boxes can be arrayed vertically for extra SPL/coverage; nominal 8-ohm load per enclosure, so parallel pairs present 4 ohms - keep cable runs short to preserve damping factor.
source_manual: 112XT / 115XT / 115XT HiQ XT LINE Operator Manual V2 (EN) and 115XT Datasheet (EN)
